title: ElderFund
text:
ElderFund - SupportGoWhere
ElderFund
Share link
Provides discretionary monthly cash payout to lower-income Singapore Citizens with severe disability, who are not able to benefit from Careshield Life, ElderShield and the Interim Disability Assistance Programme for the Elderly (IDAPE) and have low MediSave balances and inadequate personal savings to meet their long-term care needs.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
Up to $250 cash per month
Apply now
Collapse all sections
About this support

Eligible Singapore Citizens can receive up to $250 cash payout per month for as long as they remain eligible, and meet the criteria for receipt of payment.

Who is this for?

The care recipient:

Is a Singapore Citizen aged 30 and above

Lives in Singapore

Has a monthly household income per capita of $1,500 or less

Has MedisSave balance of less than $10,000

Assessed by an MOH-accredited assessor to be unable to perform 3 out of the following 6 Activities of Daily Living (ADLs):

Washing

Dressing

Feeding

Toileting

Walking or moving around

Transferring

Not a CareShield Life or ElderShield policyholder

Not a CareShield Life or ElderShield supplement plan policyholder

No longer an active recipient of ElderShield claims (including supplement claims)

Not eligible for IDAPE, or no longer an active recipient of IDAPE



^Applications from Singaporeans who do not meet the guiding financial criteria but have difficulty meeting their long-term care needs will be considered on a case-by-case basis.

How to apply?

Contact an MOH-accredited severe disability assessor for a severe disability assessment. See list of assessors.

Log in to AIC’s eService portal (eFASS) with your Singpass to submit a claim application

Where can I find help?

View more details

AIC hotline: 1800 650 6060

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry

Visit the AIC Link branch nearest to you

Scheme last updated 23 Sep 2025
SUPPORT RECOMMENDER
Answer a few questions to find schemes and services you may be eligible for.
Check now
